title: Autodesk (ADSK) Economic Prospect Score — Opus 4.6 Analysis
description: Opus 4.6 forward-looking economic prospect analysis of Autodesk, Inc. (ADSK), scoring competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment signals.
verdictDetail: Autodesk is the dominant provider of design software for architecture, engineering, construction (AEC), and manufacturing, with AutoCAD and Revit as industry standards. The company has successfully transitioned to a subscription model with 98%+ recurring revenue. However, Autodesk has been dogged by an internal accounting investigation that unsettled investors, and its growth has moderated to low-teens as the construction cycle softens. Competition from Bentley Systems in infrastructure and emerging AI-powered design tools creates pressure. The stock's premium valuation requires growth re-acceleration that is not yet visible in guidance.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Autodesk's revenue growth is in low-teens driven by subscription price increases and seat expansion, but new customer acquisition has slowed. The construction market weakness is a near-term headwind.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: FY2026 revenue guidance is approximately $6.5B, growing ~11% YoY. This is solid but trails faster-growing vertical SaaS peers like Procore (~20%) and is comparable to PTC (~10%). The growth is heavily dependent on price increases and upselling to existing customers rather than net-new logo acquisition.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: AutoCAD remains the default 2D design tool globally with 70%+ market share in its category. Revit dominates BIM (Building Information Modeling) for architects and engineers. Fusion 360 is gaining in cloud-based manufacturing design. However, Bentley Systems is competitive in infrastructure, and Bluebeam/Procore are capturing construction workflow share that Autodesk covets.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Autodesk has implemented 5-10% annual price increases on subscriptions with limited churn, reflecting deep workflow dependency. However, the shift from perpetual licenses to subscriptions has created customer resentment in some segments, and aggressive pricing risks pushing smaller firms to free alternatives like FreeCAD or SketchUp.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Autodesk's AI integration (Autodesk AI) for generative design and automated drafting is promising but early-stage. The Forma platform for early-stage design is a meaningful expansion. However, the company has been criticized for underinvestment in core product innovation — long-standing bugs and performance issues in Revit and AutoCAD frustrate the user base.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Autodesk's moat is built on file format lock-in (DWG), industry-standard training (every architecture/engineering school teaches AutoCAD/Revit), and the massive switching costs of migrating project libraries. The moat is wide but could narrow if AI tools reduce the learning curve of competing products.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: AEC firms have decades of project files in DWG and RVT formats, custom templates, libraries of reusable components, and staff trained on Autodesk tools. Switching to a competitor means migrating millions of files, retraining teams, and losing workflow customizations built over years. The switching cost is measured in months of lost productivity.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The DWG file format creates a powerful de facto standard network effect — architects, engineers, contractors, and clients all need to exchange DWG files, creating a self-reinforcing requirement for AutoCAD compatibility. BIM Collaborate Pro creates team-level network effects for project collaboration. The education channel (universities teaching Autodesk) creates a pipeline network effect.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Building codes and government procurement increasingly mandate BIM deliverables, which effectively mandate Revit or equivalent tools. Autodesk holds significant patents in CAD and BIM technology. However, the open-source OpenBIM movement and IFC standard could eventually reduce Autodesk's format lock-in if adopted widely.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Autodesk generates $1.5B+ in free cash flow on a pure software model with minimal capex. Operating margins have expanded above 35% as the subscription transition matures. However, the accounting investigation has raised questions about the quality of reported free cash flow figures, creating an overhang on financial credibility.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is mixed — the business quality is recognized but the accounting investigation and growth deceleration have capped enthusiasm. A clean resolution to the investigation and construction cycle recovery are needed catalysts.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: FY2027 estimates have been trimmed slightly as growth guidance disappointed. The accounting investigation has made analysts more skeptical of management's projections. The Street wants to see clean numbers for 2-3 quarters before rebuilding confidence in Autodesk's financial reporting.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The accounting investigation overshadows the fundamental story. While the company stated it was immaterial to financials, the CFO transition and board-level review created negative sentiment. The AI design narrative is a positive offset but not enough to fully counter governance concerns. Activist investor involvement (Starboard Value) adds both risk and potential value-unlocking pressure.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: New CEO Andrew Anagnost has generally executed well on the subscription transition and margin expansion. The appointment of a new CFO signals governance improvement. Capital allocation has been buyback-heavy ($3B+ annually). Starboard Value's board involvement could accelerate operational improvements and margin expansion.

keyRisks: The internal accounting investigation, while stated as immaterial, could reveal more significant issues or result in SEC scrutiny that further erodes investor confidence in management credibility
AI-powered design tools could reduce the learning curve advantage that keeps smaller firms on Autodesk, enabling competitors to offer 'good enough' alternatives with faster onboarding

A prolonged construction downturn in commercial real estate and office building reduces demand for AEC software licenses and delays the digital construction adoption that underpins Autodesk's growth thesis
keyCatalysts: Clean resolution of the accounting investigation and several quarters of credible financial reporting could lift the governance discount and restore the stock's premium SaaS multiple
Infrastructure spending from the US Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act and similar global programs creates a multi-year demand tailwind for civil engineering and infrastructure design software
AI-powered generative design in Autodesk tools could dramatically increase productivity for architects and engineers, driving higher willingness-to-pay and platform stickiness for premium tiers
methodology: Opus 4.6 Analysis — Economic Prospect Score based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30). Each factor scored independently with specific rationale grounded in latest available financial data and market conditions as of March 2026.
